description: Srinagar, Gulmarg, Sonmarg, and Pahalgam are some of the most scenic destinations in Jammu and Kashmir, offering a blend of natural beauty, adventure, and serenity. Srinagar, the summer capital, is renowned for its iconic Dal Lake, houseboats, Mughal gardens, and the ancient Shankaracharya Temple. Gulmarg is a winter wonderland, famous for skiing, snowboarding, and the Gulmarg Gondola, providing spectacular views of the Pir Panjal range. Sonmarg, known as the "Meadow of Gold," is a paradise for trekkers and nature lovers, offering stunning views of the Thajiwas Glacier and lush meadows. Finally, Pahalgam is a peaceful town surrounded by forests and the Lidder River, known for its trekking trails, Betaab Valley, and Aru Valley, making it an ideal spot for nature walks and pony rides. Together, these destinations offer a perfect combination of adventure, tranquility, and unmatched natural beauty.

faqs.title: 1.What is the best time to visit Srinagar, Gulmarg, Sonmarg, and Pahalgam?
faqs.content: The best time to visit is during the summer months (March to June) for pleasant weather. If you’re interested in snow activities, the winter months (November to February) are ideal.

faqs.title: 2.How far are Srinagar, Gulmarg, Sonmarg, and Pahalgam from each other?
faqs.content:
Srinagar to Gulmarg: 50 km (approx. 2 hours by road)

Gulmarg to Sonmarg: 130 km (approx. 4-5 hours by road)

Sonmarg to Pahalgam: 85 km (approx. 3-4 hours by road)

Srinagar to Pahalgam: 90 km (approx. 2-3 hours by road)

faqs.title: 3.What are the must-see attractions in Srinagar?
faqs.content: Dal Lake, Mughal Gardens (Shalimar Bagh, Nishat Bagh), Shankaracharya Temple, Hazratbal Shrine, and the floating markets.

faqs.title: 4.What are the must-visit places in Gulmarg?
faqs.content: Gulmarg Gondola, Khilanmarg, Apharwat Peak, and the beautiful meadows perfect for skiing and other winter sports.

faqs.title: 5.Is it safe to travel to Srinagar, Gulmarg, Sonmarg, and Pahalgam?
faqs.content: Yes, these places are generally safe for tourists, but it’s advisable to stay updated on local conditions and follow the guidance of local authorities.
